statusHistory.note: This was a focused Guided Return contribution clarity slice that made each existing answer feel like part of the final Return Card, so the middle of the flow feels less like filling boxes and more like Luna gathering a clear place to resume.

This was a focused moonlit atmosphere slice that made Luna’s opening and Guided Return shell feel more like one quiet return place while preserving the existing local-only flow, first-impression clarity, and Rest Return Card.

This was a focused Luna first-impression clarity slice that made the opening explain the practical payoff of Guided Return: bringing one unfinished thing back into view, finding the thread, choosing a lantern, and leaving with a Return Card.

This was a focused Guided Return Return Card slice that made Rest give the user a small useful outcome from the existing five local values, so the loop feels less like collecting inputs and more like Luna holding a clear place to resume.

2026-07-13: This was a focused Guided Return coherence pass that smoothed copy and presentation seams across the existing six-stage loop while preserving the one-question Find your place prototype, answer spaces, Rest soft landing, and five-value local-only/no-network boundary.

2026-07-13: This was a focused Guided Return Rest soft-landing slice that made the ending feel less like a report and more like Luna gently holding one completed return, while preserving the six-stage flow, one-question Find your place prototype, answer spaces, and five-value local-only/no-network boundary.

2026-07-13: Completed a focused Guided Return answer-space presentation slice that made editable responses feel less like generic form fields while preserving the six-stage flow, one-question Find your place prototype, and five-value local-only/no-network boundary.

Completed a focused Find your place one-question-at-a-time prototype that tested a less form-like Guided Return interaction while preserving the six-stage flow and five-value local-only/no-network boundary.

Completed a Guided Return Luna insight/coaching slice that made the existing editable flow less form-like by adding local deterministic stage guidance, while preserving the five-value local-only/no-network boundary.

Fixed the blocking prototype button regression after the first-use invitation defaults pass by making invitation-default formatting defensive, avoiding initialization-time helper references before display updaters exist, preserving Guided Return inline editing, and adding a lightweight button-wiring/script-initialization regression test.

2026-07-13: Completed a first-use empty-state invitation copy pass that made Luna’s five default local values feel honest and usable as prompts, not fake project content, while preserving the existing fields, storage keys, Guided Return inline editing, Shape this return Apply → Undo, safe rendering, and no-network/no-new-behavior boundary.

2026-07-13: Completed a Guided Return usability slice that made the existing five local values editable inside the flow while preserving the one-project/local-only/no-network boundary, full overview reference editing mode, Shape this return Apply → Undo behavior, storage keys, input limits, safe rendering, mobile comfort, and six-stage order.

2026-07-13: Renamed the first visible Guided Return stage from Orient to Find your place and clarified that the step helps someone see the one thing they are returning to and where it stands, while preserving stage order, behavior, storage keys, input limits, and network-free guidance.

2026-07-13: Minimized the opening threshold project context into a small unfinished-thread hint so the first impression reads as moon/Luna presence, garden gate, and Return gently, while preserving project name and next action bindings, existing actions, storage keys, and Guided Return behavior.

2026-07-13: Removed the remaining visible dashboard framing from the prototype return link and current project trail so Luna points back to its opening threshold while preserving layout, CSS, JavaScript behavior, storage keys, and Guided Return behavior.

2026-07-13: Refined the mobile opening threshold in prototype.html so the headline and project context are calmer, Return gently becomes the earlier press-start focus, and Shape this return plus Open full overview have safer bottom breathing room while preserving existing behavior.

2026-07-13: Implemented the first small garden-gate opening threshold visual slice in prototype.html, foregrounding the Luna moon/title area, making Return gently the central CSS-only press-start gate action, and keeping Shape this return plus Open full overview accessible but quieter while preserving existing behavior.

2026-07-12: Fixed the blocking Guided Return mobile layout regression from the size-stability pass by replacing the clipped rigid frame and sticky mobile action overlay with non-overlapping normal-flow stage header, content, and action regions while preserving fade-only motion, visual comfort, editing clarity, five storage keys, maxlengths, safe rendering, deterministic guidance, no network calls, and the complete loop.

2026-07-12: Corrected the remaining Guided Return size-stability blocker by giving the stage panel stable desktop/mobile block sizing, keeping overflowing stage content scrollable inside the panel, stabilizing the guided action area, and preserving fade-only motion, reduced-motion behavior, visual comfort CSS, five local storage keys, maxlengths, safe rendering, deterministic guidance, and the complete loop.

2026-07-12: Corrected the motion/transition comfort blocker by replacing Guided Return directional stage motion with calmer fade-only swaps, reducing guided-entry movement, adding steadier guided stage panel sizing, strengthening reduced-motion behavior, and preserving cleanup, rapid-click protection, mobile containment, visual comfort CSS, five storage keys, maxlengths, safe rendering, deterministic guidance, Apply/Undo, Clear local edits, and the complete Guided Return loop.

2026-07-12: Corrected the mobile visual comfort blocker by calming Luna’s page background, opening threshold, overview cards, editing panels, inputs, Guided Return shell/stage panel, and buttons with fewer busy gradients, softer contrast, restrained focus states, and less glow/depth while preserving structure, behavior, five storage keys, maxlengths, mobile containment, safe rendering, deterministic guidance, Apply/Undo, Clear local edits, and the complete Guided Return loop.

2026-07-12: Corrected the full overview/editing clarity issue by making the existing five local project-context fields read as a visible editing panel with clearer local-only copy, while preserving storage keys, maxlengths, Clear local edits, Shape this return, Apply/Undo, safe rendering, deterministic guidance, no network calls, mobile containment, and the complete Guided Return loop.

2026-07-12: Completed a bounded v0.1 stabilization/coherence pass: cleaned generated Record and Rest punctuation, aligned return-path wording, preserved tested behavior, and extended static regression checks.

Made Shape this return suggestions reversible one field at a time: Use this becomes Undo, Undo restores the exact previous field value through existing persistence/display refresh behavior, unrelated fields stay unchanged, no undo history or new persistence key is added, and Luna is back at Rest.

Corrected the blocking Guided Return mobile overflow regression: long unbroken user-entered project text now wraps within the mobile shell/card/action chain instead of widening the viewport; Luna is back at Rest.

Completed the fifth meaningful v0.1 vertical slice: added Luna's curated deterministic local voice library, integrated stable context-hashed response variants into guidance reasons, clear-enough and no-suggestion messages, and Rest, added input limits for project name (80), current goal (160), One Next Action (140), Set Aside (140), and Record the Change (180), added quiet near-limit character feedback, kept all behavior local/browser-only with no API/backend/account/cost/network call/new persistence key, and preserved the complete loop, five saved project values, navigation, motion, privacy cue, and selective Use this behavior.

Completed the fourth meaningful v0.1 vertical slice: added Luna's first deterministic local return-guidance engine, exposed it through a secondary Shape this return opening action, offered reviewable suggestions for Current goal, One Next Action, Set Aside, and Record without silently overwriting fields, kept all guidance in-browser with no API/backend/account/cost/network call, preserved the existing five project-edit storage keys as the only persisted values, and left suggestion results/history unpersisted.

Completed the third meaningful v0.1 vertical slice: the complete Guided Return loop now responds to the current one-project context, adds exactly two local values (`luna.prototype.setAside` and `luna.prototype.recordChange`), explicitly clears all five local project-edit keys, keeps Set Aside as one value rather than a backlog and Record as one brief value rather than a log, removes recipe-specific guided leakage where current context should appear, and preserves the opening threshold, Return gently action, navigation loop, motion, mobile treatment, full overview, and six-stage shell.

Completed the second meaningful v0.1 vertical slice after the opening threshold: Guided Return now carries the first full Luna loop through Orient → Remember → Choose → Set Aside → Record → Rest, preserves the opening threshold, full overview, saved values, and navigation loop, keeps Ignore For Now from becoming a backlog, keeps Record brief, and treats Rest as a real stopping point before returning to Luna.

Added one clear full-overview Return to Luna action after human testing found the opening threshold passed strongly but the full overview lacked a direct path back to the Luna opening threshold; this completes the basic navigation loop while preserving editable values and existing localStorage behavior.

Implemented the first v0.1-oriented Luna opening threshold vertical slice: the prototype now opens with a calm Luna identity threshold, a primary Return gently action into the existing Guided Return shell, quiet access to the full overview/reference editing mode, and preserved one-project storage and guided-flow behavior while leaving Set Aside, Record, and Rest intentionally unimplemented.

Implemented one bounded Guided Return app-shell experiment for Orient → Remember → Choose: a stable centered shell with persistent header, stage-content region, and predictable action area, preserving the full overview reference mode, existing motion, mobile action findings, localStorage behavior, and One Next Action lantern emphasis.

Added the Guided Return app-shell experiment boundary, recorded that existing Guided Return experiments produced enough evidence to stop automatic micro-polish, preserved the current prototype and full overview as references, and set the next direction to human-reviewing one bounded shell experiment for Orient → Remember → Choose.

Recorded the Guided Return entrance as a qualified human-test pass: it feels mostly softer and less abrupt, needs no further entrance-motion polish without a new usability finding, and preserves the One Next Action gradient as intentional lantern emphasis while keeping a quieter shared-stage visual-language observation for later.

Implemented one restrained Guided Return entrance experiment: the full overview softly yields, the focused Guided Return surface arrives as a single visual unit, reduced motion enters immediately, and the corrected mobile action layout remains closed to further polish without a new human usability finding.

Recorded that the corrected Guided Return mobile action layout passed its initial human test, needs no further action-layout polish now, and leaves the abrupt Begin a guided return entrance as a separate possible follow-up.

Replaced the oversized mobile Guided Return stage-footprint approach with natural-height stage content and a clearer mobile action layout after human testing found the tall shared min-height created empty space, did not prevent scrolling, and left Forward / Back / Return stacked unintuitively.
Darkened only the outer Guided Return container after human testing found the first dark-surface follow-up corrected the inner panel but left the larger guided area too bright.
Darkened the Guided Return stage panel surface after human testing found the first panel-level transition made the guided area brighter than the surrounding moonlit prototype.
Replaced the first quick Guided Return micro-transition with a calmer app-like panel-level transition after human testing found the 220ms content shift too quick and visually uncomfortable.

currentGoal: Rest after the Guided Return contribution clarity slice.
lastCompletedRun: Guided Return contribution clarity slice: made each existing answer feel like part of the final Return Card so the middle of the flow feels less like filling boxes.
nextStep: Human mobile test of whether each Guided Return stage feels like it contributes to the Return Card.
dashboardGuide: Welcome starts the path to read through Record the Change before choosing more work.
Status gives a quick snapshot of how the project is doing right now.
Current Mode, Current Goal, and Last Completed Run explain where Luna is paused.
Next Action highlights the one clear thing to do next.
Ignore For Now names what can wait.
Record the Change explains how to update the public project trail after one small useful change, then rest.
Optional Reflection can wait; Philosophy Garden is a gentle reflection layer, not Luna's main engine.
futureIdeas: Implement the first small garden-gate opening threshold slice.
Keep the implementation narrow: opening threshold visual/layout only, with no behavior expansion.
decisionLog: 2026-07-13: This was a focused Guided Return contribution clarity slice that made each existing answer feel like part of the final Return Card, so the middle of the flow feels less like filling boxes and more like Luna gathering a clear place to resume.

2026-07-13: This was a focused moonlit atmosphere slice that made Luna’s opening and Guided Return shell feel more like one quiet return place while preserving the existing local-only flow, first-impression clarity, and Rest Return Card.

2026-07-12: Completed the first full Guided Return loop as the second meaningful v0.1 vertical slice after the opening threshold; Set Aside stays not-a-backlog, Record stays brief, and Rest becomes a real stopping point before returning to Luna.

2026-07-12: Human testing found the new Luna opening threshold passed strongly, but returning from Guided Return to the full overview left no direct route back to the Luna opening threshold; adding one secondary Return to Luna action completes the basic navigation loop without adding competing actions or changing persistence.

2026-07-12: Luna deliberately shifted from many tiny micro-experiments toward fewer meaningful v0.1 vertical slices; the first is a real opening threshold that routes primarily into the existing Guided Return shell while preserving the full overview as quieter reference/editing mode.

2026-07-12: Implemented the first Guided Return app-shell experiment as one stable centered shell with persistent header, stage-content region, and action area for Orient → Remember → Choose; next is human mobile testing of whether it feels like one calm focused app space.

2026-07-12: The Guided Return app-shell experiment boundary became the next product direction; existing experiments produced enough evidence to stop automatic micro-polish and test the container / spatial experience next.

2026-07-12: The abrupt Begin a guided return entrance became the focused implementation experiment; the overview now softly yields before the Guided Return surface arrives as one visual unit, while mobile action layout and Forward / Back panel motion stay preserved.

2026-07-12: The corrected Guided Return mobile action layout passed its initial human test; no further action-layout polish is needed now without a new human usability finding, and the abrupt Begin a guided return entrance is a separate possible follow-up.

2026-07-11: The first Guided Return dark-surface follow-up corrected the inner panel, but the outer guided container still appeared too bright; this follow-up should only darken `.guided-return-view` while preserving the panel-level motion experiment.
2026-07-11: The first Guided Return human test passed technically; the follow-up should only improve subtle button depth so controls stand apart while remaining calm and moonlit.

2026-07-11: Guided Return is the next phase direction after the completed prototype phase; it should explore a calm guided path through part of the one-project return loop while preserving the full overview.
A dashboard makes Luna easy to understand at a glance.
Structured project data keeps updates small, safe, and beginner-friendly.
Showing goals, ideas, and decisions first creates a clear trail before adding larger features.
The Next Action card helps beginners choose one clear next move without sorting through every project note.
The Philosophy Garden seed gives Luna one tiny creative direction while staying public-safe and beginner-friendly.
The first Reflection Card gives visitors one gentle question to consider without asking for private personal details.
The Garden Path keeps Philosophy Garden updates slow, public-safe, and easy for beginners to follow.
The Garden Review helps Luna learn from the first Reflection Card before choosing the next gentle public-safe update.
The Garden Cycle connects the Reflection Card, Garden Review, Next Garden Update, and rest step so beginners can see how Philosophy Garden pieces fit together.
ideaQueue: Implement the first small garden-gate opening threshold slice.
Keep the implementation narrow: opening threshold visual/layout only, with no behavior expansion.
ideaQueueGuide: Choose one small task that can be finished clearly in a single update.
Prefer beginner-friendly changes that make Luna easier to understand.
Keep private details out of the public repo.

glossary.meaning: A project folder that keeps files and their change history together.
A safe workspace for trying changes without changing the main version right away.
A request to review and bring a set of changes into the project.
To combine approved changes from one branch into another branch.
The one safest small task Luna suggests doing next, so beginners do not have to choose from every idea at once.

localSetup: Open a terminal in the Luna project folder.
Start a simple local web server with python3 -m http.server 8000.
Visit http://localhost:8000 in a browser to view the dashboard.

nextAction.title: Human mobile Return Card contribution test
nextAction.description: Human mobile test of whether each Guided Return stage feels like it contributes to the Return Card.
nextAction.whyItMatters: The contribution clarity slice is complete; a mobile human pass should confirm each Guided Return stage feels like it is gathering a useful Return Card piece without crowding the calm local-only flow.

creativeSeed.name: Philosophy Garden
creativeSeed.description: A tiny public-safe space for simple reflection prompts, gentle questions, and beginner-friendly ideas.
ideas: Write one calm question about how a small project grows.
Add a tiny reflection card about choosing the next kind action.
Collect one beginner-friendly thought experiment with no personal details.

reflectionCard.title: Notice the Small Gate
reflectionCard.question: What is one small, kind step that could help this project grow without rushing?
reflectionCard.whyItMatters: This question matters because gentle growth helps Luna stay clear, welcoming, and easy for beginners to follow.
reflectionCard.beginnerNote: Answer with a general project idea or kind next step; do not include private personal details.

gardenReview.title: Review the Small Gate
gardenReview.lesson: The first Reflection Card teaches that Luna can grow through one small, kind step instead of rushing into bigger changes.
gardenReview.nextUpdate: Next, choose one gentle public-safe update that keeps the Philosophy Garden easy for beginners to follow.

nextGardenUpdate.title: Name One Gentle Growth Step
nextGardenUpdate.purpose: Help visitors turn the Garden Review lesson into one small, kind project action without rushing the Philosophy Garden.
nextGardenUpdate.suggestedAction: Choose one general, public-safe improvement that makes the dashboard easier for beginners to follow.

gardenCycle.title: Reflection Card
gardenCycle.sentence: Start with one gentle public-safe question for the project.

gardenCycle.title: Garden Review
gardenCycle.sentence: Review what the reflection card teaches in simple beginner-friendly words.

gardenCycle.title: Next Garden Update
gardenCycle.sentence: Choose one small public-safe action that grows from the review.

gardenCycle.title: Rest Before Growing Again
gardenCycle.sentence: Let the garden rest before adding another prompt or reflection card.

gardenPath: Plant one small public-safe reflection card.
Let the card rest before changing the garden again.
Review what the card teaches in simple beginner-friendly words.
Choose one gentle update for the next small growth step.

welcome.title: Welcome to the Luna prototype
welcome.sentence: Luna uses this repo as its first example project: see what changed, choose one clear Next Action, notice what can wait, and rest before adding more.

ignoreForNow: Do not add another dashboard section until this return path still feels calm.
Do not start backend, accounts, pricing, authentication, database, or app architecture yet.
Do not add future visual ideas, additional examples, or new planning notes until the current dashboard rests.
recordTheChange: After one small useful change, update STATE.md, CHANGELOG.md, and project.json together.
Name what changed in plain language so the next return is easier.
This is the public project trail, not a logging system, database, changelog editor, or task tracker.
Then rest before adding another dashboard section, feature, or planning note.

currentMode.name: Rest
currentMode.description: Luna is resting after the Guided Return contribution clarity slice.
currentMode.whyItMatters: The existing Guided Return stages now show how each answer contributes to the final Return Card while preserving the protected local-only flow and unchanged behavior.

nextSuggestedStep: Human mobile test of whether each Guided Return stage feels like it contributes to the Return Card.
